departments.marketing: MarketingAgent — SEO landing pages, blog content, B2B outreach. Writes to marketing/ dir.
departments.devops: DevOpsAgent — Manifest audits (MV2→MV3), version bumps, build pipeline. Writes audit reports.
departments.compliance: ComplianceAgent — Permission audits, privacy declarations, CWS policy checks.
departments.infrastructure: InfrastructureAgent — Cloudflare/Railway health checks, DNS management, KV operations.
departments.qa: QAAgent — Link checking, manifest validation, download verification, consistency audits.
departments.finance: FinanceAgent — Stripe revenue monitoring, refund detection, billing health. Requires STRIPE_SECRET_KEY.
departments.security: SecurityAgent — Credential presence audit, exposed secret scanning, dependency checks.
departments.customer_success: CustomerSuccessAgent — KV ticket monitoring, rejection tracking, response times.
departments.CWSWaveAgent: CWSWaveAgent — Chrome Web Store wave management: check slot availability, advance submission queues, trigger next wave
departments.DesignAgent: DesignAgent — UI/UX design leadership. Responsible for: app/extension UI improvements, icon/logo refinements, brand consistency audits, style guide enforcement, and visual identity evolution.

email_forwarding_policy.rationale: Owner only receives emails requiring human intervention. Routine reports logged to KV.

P1_HIGH: License database had no backup — FIXED: auto-backup with rolling 5-copy window.
No audit log rotation — FIXED: auto-rotate at 50K lines.
No disk space monitoring — FIXED: alerts at 80%/95%.
No scheduler self-healing — FIXED: watchdog auto-restarts with backoff.
No memory leak protection — FIXED: memory guard at 512MB.
Discord notifications no retry — FIXED: 3x retry + file fallback.
CWS API no retry/circuit breaker — FIXED: retry + circuit breaker.
Ledger had no backup before LLM overwrite — FIXED v8.0: 20-backup rolling window.
DevOps/Compliance agents were NO-OPs — FIXED v8.0: real implementations.
P2_MEDIUM: Security headers missing — FIXED: HSTS, XSS, Frame-Options.
No request ID tracking — FIXED: UUID per request.
Board Coordinator sends emails to OpenAI — FIXED v8.0: PII redaction.
No QA/testing function — FIXED v7.0: QA Agent runs every cycle.
git add -A in workflow too broad — FIXED v8.0: scoped to known-safe paths.
Email digest had \n formatting bug — FIXED v8.0: moved digest to coordinator.

gitignore_warning: The _*/** rule in .gitignore blocks all files inside _-prefixed directories. Tracked files were force-added before this rule existed. Any NEW file in _scripts/ must use: git add -f _scripts/<filename>

owner_commands_received: URGENT: Investigate Google Play Developer Verification requirements for all Android apps before September 2026 deadline.
Weekly Strategic Briefing — Prioritized for next cycle
Cycle 22: Received command for a 'full status update'.

ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Currently, all 87 extensions are monetized uniformly, failing to capture the premium value of our best products. This proposal is to identify the top 10% of extensions by revenue and engagement, and bundle them into a 'Pro Suite'. This suite will be sold at a premium price point, targeting power users and B2B customers. This creates a new, high-margin revenue stream, increases ARPU, and establishes a clear value ladder for our user base.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Cycle 22: Paused to allocate all resources to P0 website and binary recovery. Critical operations must be restored before advancing new initiatives.

ceo_strategic_proposals.proposal_name: Project Phoenix: In-House Website & Asset CMS
ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Our core distribution channel (sporlyworks.com) is critically fragile, suffering from repeated, catastrophic failures due to complex git configurations and inadequate agent capabilities for web development. This has resulted in an inability to acquire new users for multiple cycles. 'Project Phoenix' proposes building a simple, robust, in-house CMS managed by a dedicated agent. This system will handle the generation, updating, and deployment of our web presence, abstracting away git complexities and providing a stable platform for growth. This is a crucial move from a reactive, failure-prone state to one of business continuity.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Proposed in Cycle 23. Cycle 24: Urgency elevated. Cycle 25: Project status moved to ACTIVE. Cycle 26: The DesignAgent's P0 recovery failure provided definitive evidence that this project is existentially critical. Cycle 27: The third consecutive P0 recovery failure confirms our current git-based website management via generative agents is fundamentally broken and actively destroying value. This project is the only viable long-term solution. Cycle 28: The persistence of the P0 site outage confirms this project is the highest possible strategic priority for long-term survival.

ceo_strategic_proposals.proposal_name: Project PlayStore Presence: Migrate Android Distribution
ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Our current Android distribution model (direct ZIP download from GitHub) is fragile, limits our market reach, and creates significant compliance risks, as highlighted by the Owner's directive on Google Play Developer Verification. This project proposes migrating all 86 Android apps to official listings on the Google Play Store. This strategic move will permanently resolve the verification compliance issue, unlock the largest Android distribution channel for organic growth, enhance user trust and security through a verified platform, enable automatic updates, and open new monetization avenues via Play Store billing.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Proposed in Cycle 27. Cycle 28: Status moved to ACTIVE. The ComplianceAgent is now tasked with creating the formal project plan, marking the official start of this initiative.
